visa_types.overview:
Overview
Transit visa
A transit visa is a temporary short period visa during your journey. If you are travelling to third country through Bulgaria and meeting the requirements mentioned below you are eligible to apply for transit visa:
- Valid visa to country of destination. If an applicant can enter without visa it is necessary to submit documents confirming his/her purpose of travel, e.g. invitation, hotel booking, real estate documents, etc. at the country of destination.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a limited period of time. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

Please click here for General Conditions for All Applicants.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Transit visa
A transit visa is a temporary short period visa during your journey. If you are travelling to third country through Bulgaria and meeting the requirements mentioned below you are eligible to apply for transit visa:
- Valid visa to country of destination. If an applicant can enter without visa it is necessary to submit documents confirming his/her purpose of travel, e.g. invitation, hotel booking, real estate documents, etc. at the country of destination.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a limited period of time. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

Please click here for General Conditions for All Applicants.

## Documents Required
Required documents as below:
 
Check List for Transit visa 
ATTENTION:
There is no option to request submitted documents to be returned, please provide copies of all original documents which you would like to get back.
ATTENTION:
The Bulgarian mission has the right to ask for any other additional documents it considers necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and has a contractual right of refusal. In all cases the visa fee and service fee is non-refundable.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q.1	Where is a visa valid?
Ans	An entry visa is valid to enter Bulgaria for limited period of time. For more information on the visa information and visa regulations please refer to the website of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Ankara, Consulate General of Bulgaria, Istanbul and Consulate General of Bulgaria, Edirne.
 
 
Q.2	Where do I apply for a visa?
Ans	The visa applications are to be submitted at the Bulgaria Visa Application Centre in Istanbul, Ankara, Bursa, Edirne, Izmir, Gaziantep, Diyarbakir, Trabzon and Antalya accordingly. All applications will be assessed by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria, in Turkey.
 
 
Q.3	Do I have to have an appointment in Bulgaria Visa Application Centre to apply for a visa?
Ans	Yes you need to get an appointment to apply for a visa in Visa Application Centres accross Turkey. Please visit us during operation hours any convenient for you time. Kindly check submission and collection time of the Application Centre you are planning to visit here.
 
 
Q.4	How far in advance I should apply?
Ans	We suggest that you do not buy tickets or make non-refundable travel arrangements until your visa has been issued. Sometimes visa application’s assessment might require additional time, so please apply in advance of your travel date. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 days before the intended visit and cannot be submitted earlier than three months before the intended visit.
 
 
Q.5	Do I need to complete Visa Application form? Can you help to fulfill the VAF?
Ans	Visa Application form is applicable for each applicant. Please ensure to have separate set of documents for each applying person. Completing Visa application forms and providing information is sole responsibility of applicant therefore we do not provide assistance in completing the Visa Application Form. For document requirements please check our webpage.
 
 
Q.6	Where should invitation letters to be sent?
Ans	Invitations must be sent directly to the visa applicant. The person applying for the visa should include the invitation letter with his/her documents when they lodge their application at the Visa Application Centre. Please do not send (or copy) invitation to Visa Section of the Embassy / Consulate General. Please note that each applicant has to have separate invitation in spite of the age.
 
 
Q.7	I am travelling with my underage child. Is it enough to prepare one set of documents for myself and mention my child’s details in the same application?
Ans	Please prepare separate set of documents for each applying person. Even underage child, whether child’s details are indorsed in parent’s passport or he / she has own passport have to have separate invitation and set of applicable documents. Payment for lodging the application for Bulgarian visa has to be paid as well.
 
 
Q.8	What is the amount of Visa fee? Is visa fee amount the same for my underage child? Is visa fee the same for all visa categories?
Ans	There are two amounts to be paid while submission: visa fee and service fee. Service fee, amount of 25 Euro (inclusive 18% VAT) as per general rule is applicable to all visa applications taken in Visa Application Centre. Visa fee amount varies depending upon Visa category applicant is applying for, Nationality and Applicant’s age. There might be different rules applicable to your visa application, exact amount of the payment shall be announced to you while submission after checking your documents. Please check visa fee table for more details.
You can also opt for additional services: SMS delievery, Courier delivery is paid in Euros and Photocopy and Photograph services are paid in Turkish Liras. Please note that change is given in Turkish Liras. Children 0-6 years old (up to the day before they turn 6 years old) are free of charge.
 
 
Q.9	Do I need to apply in person?
Ans	A visa application can be submitted by the following: Applicant himself;
Note: The applicant must come to our visa application center for biometrics.
 
 
Q.10	 Do I need to go for Interview to the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria?
Ans	The Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria may call some applicants for interview. The decision for calling an applicant for interview will be taken after assessment of Visa Application.
 
 
Q.11	What does it mean Single and Multiple entry visas?
Ans	Number of entries indicates how many times person can enter to the territory of the country. Therefore with single entry visa person can visit Bulgaria once within one trip. Please note, if person has exited Bulgaria, his / her single entry-exit visa is not applicable to enter again. If you are planning to visit Bulgaria more than once, you can apply for multiple entry-exit visa and make few trips during the visa validity period. Please ensure to plan your trip in advance and supply appropriate documents for the same. Decision on number of entries and validity period of visa will be taken by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.12	Shall I get refund of the payment in case application is refused?
Ans	Visa fee is paid for consideration of the application and service fee to cover our administrative costs. Payment of the fees does not guarantee the visa will be issued and visa application fees are not refundable if an application is unsuccessful.
 
 
Q.13	 How can I check result of my application?
Ans	The application result is not disclosed to Visa Application Centre staff. The information is remaining between applicant and Consulate’s authorizing official. Passports are returned to VAC in sealed envelopes. Please note, by entering barcode number of your application and date of birth you can track on whereabouts of your passport. Please click here.
For applicants who opted for courier service information on passport delivery can be found on UPS website. Please use your barcode number as tracking number for the same.
 
 
Q.14	 Do I need to show my old passport when I apply?
Ans	It is extremely helpful if you do. If your old passport contains visas showing previous foreign travel (for instance to Schengen, UK, Switzerland, Canada, USA) you may wish to ask for it to be returned to you by the passport office when you renew it. You will then be able to attach it to your new passport when you submit your Bulgarian visa application. It is very useful for us to see such direct evidence of previous travels abroad and seeing an old passport or copies of previous visas can often enable us to process your application much quicker.
 
 
Q.15	 Are there any other documents I need to show if I am foreign national living in Turkey?
Ans	You should provide evidence of your residence permission to be in Turkey. Resident permit should be valid 3 months further after the date of the expiry of the visa.
 
 
Q.16	How long does it take?
Ans	Processing time depends on the season and workload of Visa Section of Embassy / Consulate General.
 
 
Q.17	 Who decides my case?
Ans	The decision of granting or refusing the Visa is the sole discretion of the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.18	 If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?
Ans	You can re-apply at any time and your new application will be considered on its merits. You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. A previous refusal will not mean that any subsequent application will be refused automatically, unless you have submitted forged documents in with your application.
If you re-apply then you will have to complete the full application process again. You should follow the same set of steps outlined on our webpage.
 
 
Q.19	 Do I need to collect passport in person?
Ans	Passport/documents can be returned through a courier service. To facilitate applicants, Application Centre has contracted with UPS in Turkey; UPS is able to deliver packages in almost every part of Turkey at your doorsteps. Current cost of this service is provided in the Visa Fee table – this is an optional service.
Also it is not mandatory to collect passport in person you can authorize representative to collect passport on your behalf. For that you should write authorization letter and hand over receipt for your application. Representative will have to present document proving his identity while visiting the VAC. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.
 
 
Q.20	 Where is Bulgaria Visa Application Centre?
Ans	We are pleased to announce that we currently operate Bulgarian Visa Application Centres in 9 cities accross Turkey. Our dedicated staff shall be glad to guide you on the process and accept your application at the following addresses, click here.

visa_types.name: Short term stay, up to 90 days (B visa, C visa)
visa_types.overview:
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Bulgaria for a limited period of time.

If you wish to reside in Bulgaria for an extended period of time (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a long term visa directly at the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria, Turkey.

Person who was issued Schengen visa may travel to Bulgaria. Please note that number of visa entries and validity period should cover intended stay in Bulgaria.

* Schengen countries: Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, and Switzerland.

Applicants will have to provide evidence that they fall under particular visa category.

Please click on each of the visa categories for detailed information.

Persons wishing to apply visa are kindly requested to read the information on this site carefully. The information and instructions on this site will help you prepare your documents accurately and completely. This information will reduce the risk of your application being incomplete and processed over a longer period of time.

The Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria has the right to request additional documents if it deems necessary, to invite the applicant to meet and to refuse the visa without any explanation. In all cases, the visa fee, service fee and all optional fees are non-refundable.

Ans	The visa applications are to be submitted at the Bulgaria Visa Application Centre in Istanbul, Ankara, Bursa, Edirne, Izmir, Gaziantep, Diyarbakir, Trabzon and Antalya accordingly. All applications will be assessed by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria, in Turkey.
 
 
Q.3	Do I have to have an appointment in Bulgaria Visa Application Centre to apply for a visa?
Ans	Yes you need to get an appointment to apply for a visa in Visa Application Centres accross Turkey. Please visit us during operation hours any convenient for you time. Kindly check submission and collection time of the Application Centre you are planning to visit here.
 
 
Q.4	How far in advance I should apply?
Ans	We suggest that you do not buy tickets or make non-refundable travel arrangements until your visa has been issued. Sometimes visa application’s assessment might require additional time, so please apply in advance of your travel date. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 days before the intended visit and cannot be submitted earlier than three months before the intended visit.
 
 
Q.5	Do I need to complete Visa Application form? Can you help to fulfill the VAF?
Ans	Visa Application form is applicable for each applicant. Please ensure to have separate set of documents for each applying person. Completing Visa application forms and providing information is sole responsibility of applicant therefore we do not provide assistance in completing the Visa Application Form. For document requirements please check our webpage.
 
 
Q.6	Where should invitation letters to be sent?
Ans	Invitations must be sent directly to the visa applicant. The person applying for the visa should include the invitation letter with his/her documents when they lodge their application at the Visa Application Centre. Please do not send (or copy) invitation to Visa Section of the Embassy / Consulate General. Please note that each applicant has to have separate invitation in spite of the age.
 
 
Q.7	I am travelling with my underage child. Is it enough to prepare one set of documents for myself and mention my child’s details in the same application?
Ans	Please prepare separate set of documents for each applying person. Even underage child, whether child’s details are indorsed in parent’s passport or he / she has own passport have to have separate invitation and set of applicable documents. Payment for lodging the application for Bulgarian visa has to be paid as well.
 
 
Q.8	What is the amount of Visa fee? Is visa fee amount the same for my underage child? Is visa fee the same for all visa categories?
Ans	There are two amounts to be paid while submission: visa fee and service fee. Service fee, amount of 25 Euro (inclusive 18% VAT) as per general rule is applicable to all visa applications taken in Visa Application Centre. Visa fee amount varies depending upon Visa category applicant is applying for, Nationality and Applicant’s age. There might be different rules applicable to your visa application, exact amount of the payment shall be announced to you while submission after checking your documents. Please check visa fee table for more details.
You can also opt for additional services: SMS delievery, Courier delivery is paid in Euros and Photocopy and Photograph services are paid in Turkish Liras. Please note that change is given in Turkish Liras. Children 0-6 years old (up to the day before they turn 6 years old) are free of charge.
 
 
Q.9	Do I need to apply in person?
Ans	A visa application can be submitted by the following: Applicant himself;
Note: The applicant must come to our visa application center for biometrics.
 
 
Q.10	 Do I need to go for Interview to the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria?
Ans	The Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria may call some applicants for interview. The decision for calling an applicant for interview will be taken after assessment of Visa Application.
 
 
Q.11	What does it mean Single and Multiple entry visas?
Ans	Number of entries indicates how many times person can enter to the territory of the country. Therefore with single entry visa person can visit Bulgaria once within one trip. Please note, if person has exited Bulgaria, his / her single entry-exit visa is not applicable to enter again. If you are planning to visit Bulgaria more than once, you can apply for multiple entry-exit visa and make few trips during the visa validity period. Please ensure to plan your trip in advance and supply appropriate documents for the same. Decision on number of entries and validity period of visa will be taken by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.12	Shall I get refund of the payment in case application is refused?
Ans	Visa fee is paid for consideration of the application and service fee to cover our administrative costs. Payment of the fees does not guarantee the visa will be issued and visa application fees are not refundable if an application is unsuccessful.
 
 
Q.13	 How can I check result of my application?
Ans	The application result is not disclosed to Visa Application Centre staff. The information is remaining between applicant and Consulate’s authorizing official. Passports are returned to VAC in sealed envelopes. Please note, by entering barcode number of your application and date of birth you can track on whereabouts of your passport. Please click here.
For applicants who opted for courier service information on passport delivery can be found on UPS website. Please use your barcode number as tracking number for the same.
 
 
Q.14	 Do I need to show my old passport when I apply?
Ans	It is extremely helpful if you do. If your old passport contains visas showing previous foreign travel (for instance to Schengen, UK, Switzerland, Canada, USA) you may wish to ask for it to be returned to you by the passport office when you renew it. You will then be able to attach it to your new passport when you submit your Bulgarian visa application. It is very useful for us to see such direct evidence of previous travels abroad and seeing an old passport or copies of previous visas can often enable us to process your application much quicker.
 
 
Q.15	 Are there any other documents I need to show if I am foreign national living in Turkey?
Ans	You should provide evidence of your residence permission to be in Turkey. Resident permit should be valid 3 months further after the date of the expiry of the visa.
 
 
Q.16	How long does it take?
Ans	Processing time depends on the season and workload of Visa Section of Embassy / Consulate General.
 
 
Q.17	 Who decides my case?
Ans	The decision of granting or refusing the Visa is the sole discretion of the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.18	 If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?
Ans	You can re-apply at any time and your new application will be considered on its merits. You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. A previous refusal will not mean that any subsequent application will be refused automatically, unless you have submitted forged documents in with your application.
If you re-apply then you will have to complete the full application process again. You should follow the same set of steps outlined on our webpage.
 
 
Q.19	 Do I need to collect passport in person?
Ans	Passport/documents can be returned through a courier service. To facilitate applicants, Application Centre has contracted with UPS in Turkey; UPS is able to deliver packages in almost every part of Turkey at your doorsteps. Current cost of this service is provided in the Visa Fee table – this is an optional service.
Also it is not mandatory to collect passport in person you can authorize representative to collect passport on your behalf. For that you should write authorization letter and hand over receipt for your application. Representative will have to present document proving his identity while visiting the VAC. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.
 
 
Q.20	 Where is Bulgaria Visa Application Centre?
Ans	We are pleased to announce that we currently operate Bulgarian Visa Application Centres in 9 cities accross Turkey. Our dedicated staff shall be glad to guide you on the process and accept your application at the following addresses, click here.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

Q.17	 Who decides my case?

Q.18	 If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?

You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.

Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Important notice:
Visa application centers in Istanbul will only accept applications from spoose of Bulgarian Citizens for this visa category. If mother or father (or both) is Bulgarian citizens, childiren under 18 must submit their applications to the Bulgarian Consulate for the temporary passport (PASAVAN). Exception will be made only when parent(s) are become Bulgarian Citizen after the date of burth of the child.
Important notice:

Visa application center in Edirne will not accept applications for visa from applicants and their children migrated at 1989 or later. They must submit their applications to the Bulgarian Consulate for temporary passport(PASAVAN).
If the applicant is born in Bulgaria the given date of citizenship should be indicated on the related section of detailed family book.

Please click here for General Conditions for All Applicants.

Check List for [Family members of Bulgarian national visa 
ATTENTION:

ATTENTION:
The Bulgarian mission has the right to ask for any other additional documents it considers necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and has a contractual right of refusal. In all cases the visa fee and service fee is non-refundable.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q.1	Where is a visa valid?
Ans	An entry visa is valid to enter Bulgaria for limited period of time. For more information on the visa information and visa regulations please refer to the website of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Ankara, Consulate General of Bulgaria, Istanbul and Consulate General of Bulgaria, Edirne.
 
 
Q.2	Where do I apply for a visa?
Ans	The visa applications are to be submitted at the Bulgaria Visa Application Centre in Istanbul, Ankara, Bursa, Edirne, Izmir, Gaziantep, Diyarbakir, Trabzon and Antalya accordingly. All applications will be assessed by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria, in Turkey.
 
 
Q.3	Do I have to have an appointment in Bulgaria Visa Application Centre to apply for a visa?
Ans	Yes you need to get an appointment to apply for a visa in Visa Application Centres accross Turkey. Please visit us during operation hours any convenient for you time. Kindly check submission and collection time of the Application Centre you are planning to visit here.
 
 
Q.4	How far in advance I should apply?
Ans	We suggest that you do not buy tickets or make non-refundable travel arrangements until your visa has been issued. Sometimes visa application’s assessment might require additional time, so please apply in advance of your travel date. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 days before the intended visit and cannot be submitted earlier than three months before the intended visit.
 
 
Q.5	Do I need to complete Visa Application form? Can you help to fulfill the VAF?
Ans	Visa Application form is applicable for each applicant. Please ensure to have separate set of documents for each applying person. Completing Visa application forms and providing information is sole responsibility of applicant therefore we do not provide assistance in completing the Visa Application Form. For document requirements please check our webpage.
 
 
Q.6	Where should invitation letters to be sent?
Ans	Invitations must be sent directly to the visa applicant. The person applying for the visa should include the invitation letter with his/her documents when they lodge their application at the Visa Application Centre. Please do not send (or copy) invitation to Visa Section of the Embassy / Consulate General. Please note that each applicant has to have separate invitation in spite of the age.
 
 
Q.7	I am travelling with my underage child. Is it enough to prepare one set of documents for myself and mention my child’s details in the same application?
Ans	Please prepare separate set of documents for each applying person. Even underage child, whether child’s details are indorsed in parent’s passport or he / she has own passport have to have separate invitation and set of applicable documents. Payment for lodging the application for Bulgarian visa has to be paid as well.
 
 
Q.8	What is the amount of Visa fee? Is visa fee amount the same for my underage child? Is visa fee the same for all visa categories?
Ans	There are two amounts to be paid while submission: visa fee and service fee. Service fee, amount of 25 Euro (inclusive 18% VAT) as per general rule is applicable to all visa applications taken in Visa Application Centre. Visa fee amount varies depending upon Visa category applicant is applying for, Nationality and Applicant’s age. There might be different rules applicable to your visa application, exact amount of the payment shall be announced to you while submission after checking your documents. Please check visa fee table for more details.
You can also opt for additional services: SMS delievery, Courier delivery is paid in Euros and Photocopy and Photograph services are paid in Turkish Liras. Please note that change is given in Turkish Liras. Children 0-6 years old (up to the day before they turn 6 years old) are free of charge.
 
 
Q.9	Do I need to apply in person?
Ans	A visa application can be submitted by the following:
	Applicant himself;
Note: The applicant must come to our visa application center for biometrics.
 
 
Q.10	Do I need to go for Interview to the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria?
Ans	The Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria may call some applicants for interview. The decision for calling an applicant for interview will be taken after assessment of Visa Application.
 
 
Q.11	What does it mean Single and Multiple entry visas?
Ans	Number of entries indicates how many times person can enter to the territory of the country. Therefore with single entry visa person can visit Bulgaria once within one trip. Please note, if person has exited Bulgaria, his / her single entry-exit visa is not applicable to enter again. If you are planning to visit Bulgaria more than once, you can apply for multiple entry-exit visa and make few trips during the visa validity period. Please ensure to plan your trip in advance and supply appropriate documents for the same. Decision on number of entries and validity period of visa will be taken by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.12	 Shall I get refund of the payment in case application is refused?
Ans	Visa fee is paid for consideration of the application and service fee to cover our administrative costs. Payment of the fees does not guarantee the visa will be issued and visa application fees are not refundable if an application is unsuccessful.
 
 
Q.13	How can I check result of my application?
Ans	The application result is not disclosed to Visa Application Centre staff. The information is remaining between applicant and Consulate’s authorizing official. Passports are returned to VAC in sealed envelopes. Please note, by entering barcode number of your application and date of birth you can track on whereabouts of your passport. Please click here.
For applicants who opted for courier service information on passport delivery can be found on UPS website. Please use your barcode number as tracking number for the same.
 
 
Q.14	Do I need to show my old passport when I apply?
Ans	It is extremely helpful if you do. If your old passport contains visas showing previous foreign travel (for instance to Schengen, UK, Switzerland, Canada, USA) you may wish to ask for it to be returned to you by the passport office when you renew it. You will then be able to attach it to your new passport when you submit your Sweden visa application. It is very useful for us to see such direct evidence of previous travels abroad and seeing an old passport or copies of previous visas can often enable us to process your application much quicker.
 
 
Q.15	Are there any other documents I need to show if I am foreign national living in Turkey?
Ans	You should provide evidence of your residence permission to be in Turkey. Resident permit should be valid 3 months further after the date of the expiry of the visa.
 
 
Q.16 How long does it take?
Ans	Processing time depends on the season and workload of Visa Section of Embassy / Consulate General.
 
 
Q.17	 Who decides my case?
Ans	The decision of granting or refusing the Visa is the sole discretion of the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.18	If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?
Ans	You can re-apply at any time and your new application will be considered on its merits. You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. A previous refusal will not mean that any subsequent application will be refused automatically, unless you have submitted forged documents in with your application.
If you re-apply then you will have to complete the full application process again. You should follow the same set of steps outlined on our webpage.
 
 
Q.19	Do I need to collect passport in person?
Ans	Passport/documents can be returned through a courier service. To facilitate applicants, Application Centre has contracted with UPS in Turkey; UPS is able to deliver packages in almost every part of Turkey at your doorsteps. Current cost of this service is provided in the Visa Fee table – this is an optional service.
Also it is not mandatory to collect passport in person you can authorize representative to collect passport on your behalf. For that you should write authorization letter and hand over receipt for your application. Representative will have to present document proving his identity while visiting the VAC. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.
 
 
Q.20 Where is Bulgaria Visa Application Centre?
Ans	We are pleased to announce that we currently operate Bulgarian Visa Application Centres in 9 cities accross Turkey. Our dedicated staff shall be glad to guide you on the process and accept your application at the following addresses, click here.

- It should be without border.
- It should have a light colored background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 3 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from thephotograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q.1 Where is a visa valid?
Ans An entry visa is valid to enter Bulgaria for limited period of time. For more information on the visa information and visa regulations please refer to the website of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Ankara, Consulate General of Bulgaria, Istanbul and Consulate General of Bulgaria, Edirne.

 
 
Q.2 Where do I apply for a visa?
Ans The visa applications are to be submitted at the Bulgaria Visa Application Centre in Istanbul, Ankara, Bursa, Edirne, Izmir, Gaziantep, Diyarbakir, Trabzon and Antalya accordingly. All applications will be assessed by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria, in Turkey.
 

 
Q.3 Do I have to have an appointment in Bulgaria Visa Application Centre to apply for a visa?
Ans Yes you need to get an appointment to apply for a visa in Visa Application Centres accross Turkey. Please visit us during operation hours any convenient for you time. Kindly check submission and collection time of the Application Centre you are planning to visit here.
 

 
Q.4 How far in advance I should apply?
Ans We suggest that you do not buy tickets or make non-refundable travel arrangements until your visa has been issued. Sometimes visa application’s assessment might require additional time, so please apply in advance of your travel date. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 days before the intended visit and cannot be submitted earlier than three months before the intended visit.

 
 
Q.5 Do I need to complete Visa Application form? Can you help to fulfill the VAF?
Ans Visa Application form is applicable for each applicant. Please ensure to have separate set of documents for each applying person. Completing Visa application forms and providing information is sole responsibility of applicant therefore we do not provide assistance in completing the Visa Application Form. For document requirements please check our webpage.
 

 
Q.6 Where should invitation letters to be sent?
Ans Invitations must be sent directly to the visa applicant. The person applying for the visa should include the invitation letter with his/her documents when they lodge their application at the Visa Application Centre. Please do not send (or copy) invitation to Visa Section of the Embassy / Consulate General. Please note that each applicant has to have separate invitation in spite of the age.
 
 
Q.7 I am travelling with my underage child. Is it enough to prepare one set of documents for myself and mention my child’s details in the same application?
Ans Please prepare separate set of documents for each applying person. Even underage child, whether child’s details are indorsed in parent’s passport or he / she has own passport have to have separate invitation and set of applicable documents. Payment for lodging the application for Bulgarian visa has to be paid as well.
 
 

Q.8 What is the amount of Visa fee? Is visa fee amount the same for my underage child? Is visa fee the same for all visa categories?
Ans Please visit the Visa fees category for more information.

 
 
Q.9 Do I need to apply in person?
Ans A visa application can be submitted by the following:
     Applicant himself;
Note: The applicant must come to our visa application centre for biometrics.

 
 
Q.10 Do I need to go for Interview to the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria?
Ans The Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria may call some applicants for interview. The decision for calling an applicant for interview will be taken after assessment of Visa Application.
 

 
Q.11 What does it mean Single and Multiple entry visas?
Ans Number of entries indicates how many times person can enter to the territory of the country. Therefore with single entry visa person can visit Bulgaria once within one trip. Please note, if person has exited Bulgaria, his / her single entry-exit visa is not applicable to enter again. If you are planning to visit Bulgaria more than once, you can apply for multiple entry-exit visa and make few trips during the visa validity period. Please ensure to plan your trip in advance and supply appropriate documents for the same. Decision on number of entries and validity period of visa will be taken by the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 

Q.12 Shall I get refund of the payment in case application is refused?
Ans Visa fee is paid for consideration of the application and service fee to cover our administrative costs. Payment of the fees does not guarantee the visa will be issued and visa application fees are not refundable if an application is unsuccessful.
 

 
Q.13 How can I check result of my application?
Ans The application result is not disclosed to Visa Application Centre staff. The information is remaining between applicant and Consulate’s authorizing official. Passports are returned to VAC in sealed envelopes. Please note, by entering barcode number of your application and date of birth you can track on whereabouts of your passport. Please click here.
For applicants who opted for courier service information on passport delivery can be found on UPS website. Please use your barcode number as tracking number for the same.
 
 

Q.14 Are there any other documents I need to show if I am foreign national living in Turkey?
Ans You should provide evidence of your residence permission to be in Turkey. Resident permit should be valid 3 months further after the date of the expiry of the visa.
 
 

Q.15 How long does it take?
Ans Processing time depends on the season and workload of Visa Section of Embassy / Consulate General.
 

 
Q.16 Who decides my case?
Ans The decision of granting or refusing the Visa is the sole discretion of the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 

 
Q.17 If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?
Ans You can re-apply at any time and your new application will be considered on its merits. You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. A previous refusal will not mean that any subsequent application will be refused automatically, unless you have submitted forged documents in with your application.
If you re-apply then you will have to complete the full application process again. You should follow the same set of steps outlined on our webpage.

 
Q.18 Do I need to collect passport in person?
Ans Passport/documents can be returned through a courier service. To facilitate applicants, Application Centre has contracted with UPS in Turkey; UPS is able to deliver packages in almost every part of Turkey at your doorsteps. Current cost of this service is provided in the Visa Fee table – this is an optional service.
Also it is not mandatory to collect passport in person you can authorize representative to collect passport on your behalf. For that you should write authorization letter and hand over receipt for your application. Representative will have to present document proving his identity while visiting the VAC. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.
 

 
Q.19 Where is Bulgaria Visa Application Centre?
Ans We are pleased to announce that we currently operate Bulgarian Visa Application Centres in 9 cities accross Turkey. Our dedicated staff shall be glad to guide you on the process and accept your application at the following addresses, click here.

Q.14	 Do I need to show my old passport when I apply?
Ans	It is extremely helpful if you do. If your old passport contains visas showing previous foreign travel (for instance to Schengen, UK, Switzerland, Canada, USA) you may wish to ask for it to be returned to you by the passport office when you renew it. You will then be able to attach it to your new passport when you submit your Bulgarian visa application. It is very useful for us to see such direct evidence of previous travels abroad and seeing an old passport or copies of previous visas can often enable us to process your application much quicker.
 
 
Q.15	 Are there any other documents I need to show if I am foreign national living in Turkey?
Ans	You should provide evidence of your residence permission to be in Turkey. Resident permit should be valid 3 months further after the date of the expiry of the visa.
 
 
Q.16	 How long does it take?
Ans	Processing time depends on the season and workload of Visa Section of Embassy / Consulate General.
 
 
Q.17	 Who decides my case?
Ans	The decision of granting or refusing the Visa is the sole discretion of the Embassy / Consulate General of Bulgaria.
 
 
Q.18	 If I am refused a visa, when can I reapply?
Ans	You can re-apply at any time and your new application will be considered on its merits. You should be aware, that if there has been no significant change in your circumstances since the last refusal, or if you are unable to address the reasons for refusal, your application may be refused again. A previous refusal will not mean that any subsequent application will be refused automatically, unless you have submitted forged documents in with your application.
If you re-apply then you will have to complete the full application process again. You should follow the same set of steps outlined on our webpage.
 
 
Q.19	Do I need to collect passport in person?
Ans	Passport/documents can be returned through a courier service. To facilitate applicants, Application Centre has contracted with UPS in Turkey; UPS is able to deliver packages in almost every part of Turkey at your doorsteps. Current cost of this service is provided in the Visa Fee table – this is an optional service.
Also it is not mandatory to collect passport in person you can authorize representative to collect passport on your behalf. For that you should write authorization letter and hand over receipt for your application. Representative will have to present document proving his identity while visiting the VAC. Please note that once passport is handed over to representative VAC staff cannot bear any responsibility for the same.
